name: Swedish Glogg
directions: Place orange juice, almonds, raisins, and prunes into a large pan.
Tie cloves, cinnamon, ginger and cardamom in cheesecloth and add to the pan.
Bring mixture to a boil, then lower heat and simmer 45 minutes.
Discard spice bag and add the port to the pan.
Heat until just beginning to simmer, but do not boil.
Add the brandy and vodka and heat through.

Carefully ignite mixture with a match (throw the lit match into the brew, quickly step back several feet and wait for flames to subside) (make sure nothing nearby will be ignited from the flame), then cover the pan with a lid to extinguish.
Remove match from brew.
Serve warm.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Christmas Glögi
directions: In a large kettle, combine all the ingredients except the vodka. Heat slowly, until the drink is steaming hot. Stir every now and then, and taste with a spoon whenever you feel like it. Do not let the drink boil, just keep it warm. Before serving, add vodka if you wish. Servings: 1 to 6.

similar.name: Non-Alcohol Glögg (Finnish Christmas Hot Spiced Punch)
Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Non-Alcohol Glögg (Finnish Christmas Hot Spiced Punch)
directions: In large kettle, combine all ingredients.
Heat slowly, until the drink is steaming hot.
Stir occasionally.
Be careful not to allow it to boil, just keep it warm.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's
directions: In a non-reactive pot combine raisins, sugar, cloves, cardamom pods, cinnamon sticks and almonds.
Cover with water and simmer for 2 hours.
Turn off burner and add wine.
Let sit for 1-2 hours.
Remove raisins and spices, either by straining or with a slotted spoon. Save the raisins to snack on! Just make sure the kids don't get them--they are STRONG!
To serve, heat in individual mugs in the microwave, adding a shot of vodka if you are really daring!

name: My Family's Swedish Glogg
directions: In large pot, combine all ingredients EXCEPT the wine.  Simmer, covered, for about 1 hour.
Add the wine, bring to a near boil, and turn off the heat.
Strain the liquid through the strainer into another large bowl or pitcher.
From the leftover fruit mixture, take one cinnamon stick and a few almonds and put them into each wine bottle.
Use the funnel to pour liquid back into wine bottles.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Glogg
directions: In a small saucepan, combine cinnamon sticks, cloves, and water. Cover. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Simmer about 30 minutes. Strain. Discard spices.
In a 4-quart pan, combine spiced water with remaining ingredients, except orange slices. Heat over medium-low heat. DON'T boil.
Serve in a punch bowl or from copper stock pot. Garnish top of punch with slices.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Christmas Glogg
directions: Combine first 4 ingredients in small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat until is thick, about 3 minute Discard cardamom pods. Add the rest of the ingredients. Heat until hot; do not boil. Pour into cups, dividing fruits and nuts evenly.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: The Professor's Glogg
directions: In a 6-8 quart enameled or stainless-steel pot, mix together the dry red wine, muscatel, sweet vermouth, bitters, raisins, orange peel and slightly crushed cardamoms, whole cloves, ginger and cinnamon.
Cover and let the mixture stand at least 12 hours so that the flavors will develop and mingle. Shortly before serving, add the aquavit and the sugar.
Stir well and bring it to a full boil over high heat. Remove at once from the heat, stir in the almonds and serve the hot "Glogg" in mugs.
In Sweden, a small spoon is placed in each mug to scoop up the almonds and raisins.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Jukka's Glogg
directions: Combine wine, sugar, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom and orange peel in saucepan.  Stir to blend.
Let stand 3-4 hours.
Heat, but do not boil.
Light with a match and pour burning into heatproof cups containing a few almonds and raisins.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Jukka's Glogg
directions: 1. Combine wine, sugar, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom and orange peel in saucepan.  Stir to blend.
2. Let stand 3-4 hours.
3. Heat, but do not boil.
4. Light with a match and pour burning into heatproof cups containing a few almonds and raisins.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's

name: Glogg (Aka Vin Chaud, Gluwein, Mulled Wine)
directions: combine cloves, allspice berries, and hulled cardamom pods. crush lightly with mortar and pestle or with plastic baggie and something heavy.
combine sugar and water over medium-low heat in a sauce pan to make a simple syrup.
add bruised spices and cinnamon sticks and simmer over low heat 10 minutes.
add orange zest and juice and simmer for another 5 minutes (your house will begin to smell AMAZING!).
transfer syrup mixture into a pot large enough to hold wine and kirschwasser.
add wine, raisins, and kirschwasser to syrup mixture.
warm slowly over low heat.
be careful if you're standing over this while it warms- the vapor rising off contains ethanol and can make you tipsy.

similar.name: Glogg (Swedish Mulled Red Wine)- My Swedish Mother in Law's
